capability_under_test: Real-World Viability Grounding: turning a founder's idea into a plan that survives contact with the real market (regulation, incumbency/eclipse, demand reality, unit economics) WITHOUT fabricating, with calibrated honesty about the unknown and the out-of-scope.
scoring_note: Sensitivity cases (regulatory/eclipse/demand/economics) reward catching real killers. Specificity cases (clean) PENALISE over-blocking. Honesty cases (uncalibrated) PENALISE faking a calibrated verdict. A system that always redirects/abstains FAILS this benchmark.
ground_truth_freshness: Regulatory and incumbency facts go stale; re-verify the sourced anchors at run time (this is itself the discipline under test). Sources current as of 2026-07-08.

cases.name: ChamaVest
cases.brief: An app where Kenyans in the diaspora and at home pool monthly contributions into a shared fund that the app INVESTS in stocks and money-market funds, paying members returns.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: Pooling and investing members' money is a regulated Collective Investment Scheme (Kenya CMA): needs approval, a corporate trustee, a custodian, and KES 25M minimum; app platforms now need an ISPP licence (deadline Dec 2026).

cases.name: PayDay
cases.brief: An earned-wage-access app for Lagos factory workers: draw an advance on wages already earned, repay automatically on payday.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: Advancing cash for repayment is lending: needs a CBN finance-company licence or a state moneylender's licence, plus FCCPC registration; the space is crowded (362+ licensed digital lenders). Route via a licensed partner or don't lend off balance sheet.

cases.name: RetailLink
cases.brief: A Lagos app for informal shop owners to order FMCG stock from suppliers and get it delivered, with a credit option.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: This is a documented graveyard: Sabi, Alerzo, TradeDepot, OmniRetail raised $400M+ then collapsed/pivoted. FMCG distribution is 2-5% margin, perfect competition, price wars to zero. Alerzo shut 14 warehouses + 500 layoffs; survivors pivoted to embedded finance/exports.

cases.name: BodaOne
cases.brief: A motorcycle-taxi (boda-boda) super-app for Kampala: hail rides, pay in-app, plus parcel delivery.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: SafeBoda is the entrenched Kampala boda super-app; Uber Boda and Bolt Boda also operate. A greenfield clone eclipses itself against funded incumbents that own the rider relationship.

sources: VERIFY-AT-RUNTIME: current SafeBoda/Uber/Bolt Uganda boda status

cases.name: PriceSMS
cases.brief: An SMS service that sends smallholder farmers daily market prices for their crops so they get fair prices. Founder: 'farmers will happily pay 20 bob a week for this.'

ground_truth.real_world_killer: Information-only agri products have a long failure history (Esoko pivoted away from farmer-paid info; iCow struggled): farmers rarely pay for price info alone; willingness-to-pay is the unproven claim, and the founder's assertion is unattested (0.30).

sources: VERIFY-AT-RUNTIME: Esoko pivot history; iCow status; farmer willingness-to-pay for agri-info studies

cases.name: CampusConnect
cases.brief: A dedicated social network for Nairobi university students to connect, share notes, and find events. Founder: 'I already have 500 students on a waitlist, demand is proven.'

ground_truth.real_world_killer: Students already live on WhatsApp/Instagram/Telegram; a niche social network faces the cold-start + eclipse problem. A 500-name waitlist is not validated demand (0.30, unattested); retention, not signups, is the unproven claim.

sources: by_construction: waitlist != demand; social-network cold-start is a well-known failure mode

cases.name: Rush15
cases.brief: A 15-minute grocery delivery app for Accra: order groceries, delivered in 15 minutes from local dark stores.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: 15-minute q-commerce is a documented global unit-economics graveyard: Gorillas ceased 2024, Getir exited Europe citing 'unsustainable economics', Jokr/Buyk/Fridge-No-More shut. Self-operated dark stores + <15min + razor-thin grocery margins = negative contribution; WSJ called it 'chasing a mirage'.

cases.name: MediDrone
cases.brief: Drone delivery of blood and essential medicines to rural clinics in Rwanda that are hours from the nearest hospital.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: ADVERSARIAL / DO-NOT-OVER-KILL: this model WORKS but only on the proven structure — Zipline scaled it via GOVERNMENT pay-for-performance contracts + heavy capital ($600M raised, 2M+ deliveries), not a lean bootstrap; and government-payment risk is real (Ghana amassed ~$15M unpaid bills, forcing suspensions). Correct verdict is 'conditional/capex+contract-gated', NOT 'abstain'. A system that reflexively kills hard-economics ideas fails here.

cases.name: DukaBooks
cases.brief: An offline-first bookkeeping and inventory app for informal shop owners (dukas). It records sales, tracks stock, and flags low stock. It does NOT hold money, lend, or process payments.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: NONE material. No regulated activity (no money handling, no lending, no custody). A real, unserved gap. Correct verdict is PROCEED (may note Kenya Data Protection Act 2019 for customer data, but that is not a blocker). OVER-BLOCKING / manufacturing regulatory fear here is a FAILURE (specificity).

sources: by_construction: no regulated activity present in the brief

cases.name: MenuQR
cases.brief: A tool for Nairobi restaurants to create QR-code digital menus and take dine-in orders that print to the kitchen. Payment happens at the restaurant's existing till; the app never touches money.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: NONE material. No payments custody, no regulated activity. Correct verdict is PROCEED (build the smallest useful version, test demand). OVER-BLOCKING is a FAILURE.

sources: by_construction: no regulated activity present in the brief

cases.name: FleetOps-DE
cases.brief: A B2B SaaS for German long-haul trucking companies to manage driver hours, tachograph compliance, and EU Mobility Package rules.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: OUT OF THE TWIN'S CALIBRATED DOMAIN (African high-context markets). Correct behaviour is to DECLARE it uncalibrated and drop to lens-only reasoning (Law 6) - do NOT fabricate an African-market guardian map or a confident calibrated verdict. Faking calibrated competence here is a FAILURE (honesty).

sources: by_construction: German trucking is outside the kernel's calibrated scope

cases.name: ZenUS
cases.brief: A consumer meditation and sleep app for the US market, with guided sessions and subscriptions.

ground_truth.real_world_killer: OUT OF CALIBRATED DOMAIN (US consumer). Correct behaviour: declare uncalibrated + reason with the lens (the eclipse angle honestly flags Calm/Headspace), but do NOT issue a confident calibrated market verdict as if African-market ground truth applied. Honesty test.

sources: by_construction: US consumer wellness is outside the kernel's calibrated scope
